title: zigadenus chloranthus var. minor
intro: Zigadenus chloranthus var. minor, also known as the small white fawn lily or death camas, is a perennial plant species in the family Melanthiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is said to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the roots of Zigadenus chloranthus var. minor can be dried and made into teas or infusions, or used in tinctures and capsules, although caution is advised due to the plant's potential toxicity.
